claim(uint256).details: Uses originalDeposited (not totalDeposited) for proportional math.      Accounts for emergency withdrawals by using the effective pool.

emergencyWithdraw(uint256).details: totalDeposited is NOT modified — totalEmergencyWithdrawn tracks separately.      This preserves proportional math for remaining claimers after settlement.

settleSignal(uint256).details: Uses balanceAtOpen snapshot to detect manipulation via direct transfers

cancelSignal().notice: Cancel a signal — full refund (COLLECTING phase only)

claim(uint256).notice: Claim proportional share of returned USDC

closeTrade(uint32,uint64).notice: Step 2b: Close a gTrade position via the contract

deposit(uint256).notice: Deposit USDC into the current signal pool

emergencyWithdraw(uint256).notice: Emergency withdraw if signal stays in TRADING for 7+ days

openTrade(uint32).notice: Step 2: Open the gTrade position with the total pool

postSignal(bool,uint64,uint64,uint64,uin.notice: Step 1: Post a signal — users can now deposit

settleSignal(uint256).notice: Step 3: Settle — record actual USDC returned from gTrade

userCancelExpiredSignal(uint256).notice: Anyone can cancel a signal if COLLECTING phase exceeds timeout

withdrawDeposit(uint256).notice: Withdraw deposit before trade opens (COLLECTING phase only)
